issue.title: Retire next large inline raw adapter body

artifacts.role: WordPress-profile adapter template renderer and WP_Http_Cookie constructor template use
compiler-owned bounded WP_Http_Cookie constructor public adapter template
runner requiring adapter template provenance before accepting WPHX-312.53 generated shell
candidate manifest recording cookie constructor adapter template shape checks
public-shell snapshots regenerated with cookie constructor adapter template manifest field

claims: The bounded WP_Http_Cookie::__construct public adapter body no longer lives as a large inline Haxe string concatenation.
The WPHX-312.53 runner requires template path, hash, placeholder list, ownership, and upstream reference evidence for the generated cookie shell.
PHP still owns constructor parsing/defaults while Haxe owns only bounded post-construction cookie matching, header formatting, and attribute shape.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http_Cookie constructor ownership or broader cookie propagation ownership.
This does not claim Requests cookie jar semantics, live HTTP behavior, or installed WordPress distribution behavior.
This does not claim WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
